reasoning: A net reduction of 250,000 federal employees in a single year (2025) is equivalent to shrinking the ~$2.9 million federal civilian workforce by over 8.5%. Even with intense political will from Trump and Elon Musk, executing such a reduction within 11 months is administratively and legally arduous. Title 5 civil service protections require lengthy procedures for Reductions in Force (RIFs), which would inevitably face court injunctions and union opposition. Furthermore, the vast majority of federal employees work in the DoD, VA, and DHS—areas traditionally shielded from mass cuts by Republicans. While a strict hiring freeze and natural attrition (~6% annually) could yield around 100,000 to 150,000 departures without replacement, reaching >250,000 would require unprecedented buyouts or controversial mass firings that are improbable to materialize and survive legal challenges within the 2025 calendar year.
